assets.abstract: This review synthesizes current understanding of metacognitive processes across neuroscientific, clinical, and educational domains. Metacognition, defined broadly to encompass knowledge/awareness, and monitoring/regulation of one's cognitive processes spans both neurological and psychological domains. This quality holds significant implications for human development. Neuroimaging evidence suggests that metacognition relies on distributed networks spanning prefrontal-parietal circuits, with conne

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Metacognition — self-monitoring, metacognitive awareness, prefrontal-parietal insight circuits.
